methods.method_name: Plant Growth and Transgenic Line Generation
methods.description: This method describes the growth conditions for plant material and the generation of transgenic plant lines using Gateway technology.

steps.description: Seeds were sown on half-strength MS medium with 1% sucrose and 1% agar, stratified for 2 d at 4°C and grown for 7 d in short-day conditions (8 h light/16 h dark).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and stratify seeds on MS medium for initial growth

steps.description: Seedlings were then transferred to soil and grown for 3 more weeks in short-day conditions (8 h light/16 h dark) and subsequently transferred to long-day conditions (16 h light/8 h dark) to promote flowering.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer seedlings to soil and adjust light conditions to promote flowering

steps.description: Gateway technology was used for the production of genetic constructs.
steps.searchable_summary: Use Gateway technology to create genetic constructs

steps.description: Constructs were transformed into the Col-0 background.
steps.searchable_summary: Transform genetic constructs into Col-0 plant background

methods.description: This method outlines the procedures for histological analysis and microscopy of plant tissues.

steps.description: Inflorescences were fixed in 50% ethanol, 5% acetic acid, 3.7% formaldehyde overnight; dehydrated in a graded series of ethanol.
steps.searchable_summary: Fix and dehydrate plant inflorescences for histological analysis

steps.description: Samples were infiltrated in Technovit 7100 infiltration solution with vacuum for 2 h and further incubated for 6 d.
steps.searchable_summary: Infiltrate samples with Technovit 7100 under vacuum

steps.description: Samples were polymerized with Technovit 7100 polymerization solution at RT for 6 h.
Sections were stained with acriflavine or toluidine blue and observed using confocal or light microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: Stain and observe sections using microscopy

methods.description: This method describes the use of scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and cryo-SEM for observing pollen grains and anthers.

steps.description: Pollen grains were observed using an FEI FEG Quanta 250 SEM at 5-kV voltage.
Anthers were immobilized on a specimen mount, rapidly frozen in liquid nitrogen, and observed using cryo-SEM.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and observe anthers using cryo-SEM

methods.description: This method outlines the preparation and observation of plant samples using transmission electron microscopy.

steps.description: Flower buds were fixed with formaldehyde and glutaraldehyde under vacuum at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Fix flower buds for TEM analysis

steps.description: Samples were postfixed with osmium tetroxide and dehydrated in a graded ethanol series.
steps.searchable_summary: Postfix and dehydrate samples for TEM

steps.description: Samples were infiltrated with Spurr resin and polymerized at 60°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Infiltrate and polymerize samples with Spurr resin

steps.description: Ultrathin sections were poststained and examined under TEM.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and observe ultrathin sections using TEM
